prep.notes:
Marinate chicken cubes overnight in yogurt + spices.
Air fry 3 min, rotate, 3 min, check for white inside, +2 min as needed.
Yields 2 meal-prep containers, each 1 serving.

Marinate chicken cubes overnight in yogurt + spices.
Air fry 3 min, rotate, 3 min, check for white inside, +2 min as needed.
Breast cooks faster and dries out — pull as soon as interior is white.
Yields 2 meal-prep containers, each 1 serving.

Air fry chicken thighs 4-6 min; cube.
Mix chicken thoroughly with one full can of refried beans.
Divide filling into 4 meal-prep containers.
Add 25 g shredded cheese to each container.
To eat: roll one container's filling into a burrito tortilla,
air fry the wrapped burrito for 6 min.

Mix ground turkey, panko, finely-cut onions, fresh chilli, and coriander.
Form into 12 patties; arrange on a tray.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Bake 12 min total (6 min each side).
Check internal temp reaches 165°F with a thermometer.
Freeze for later.
1 patty = 1 serving.

AHEAD (overnight ideal):
1. Rice: rinse basmati until water runs clear. Instant Pot with 225g water, High pressure 5 min, natural release 10 min. Spread on tray, refrigerate uncovered.
2. Chicken: toss diced thigh with ginger-garlic paste, soy, sesame oil, and ½ of the white pepper. Rest 20–30 min (or marinate overnight). Spread single-layer on lined sheet pan. Roast at 220°C / 425°F for ~18 min until browned. Cool.
3. Veg: spread frozen mixed veg on a tray from frozen — do not thaw. Roast at 220°C / 425°F for 15–18 min until water cooks off and edges brown. Cool.

EAT DAY (~6 min):
4. Heat pan or Instant Pot (Sauté More/High) ripping hot. Add olive oil.
5. Add cold rice, break up clumps, spread single layer. Let sit ~1 min, toss, repeat 2–3 min until toasty grains appear.
6. Pour soy sauce around the edge so it sizzles. Toss to coat. Add remaining white pepper. Taste before adding more soy.
7. Fold in roasted chicken and roasted veg. Toss 1–2 min to heat. Kill heat, drizzle finishing sesame oil, toss once, portion.

Notes: Cold rice is essential — fries crisp instead of clumping. Storage: 4 days fridge. Reheat in hot skillet or under broiler, not microwave.
